course_description:
Introduction to basic skills of communication in French language. Open only to students with no previous experience of French. Part 1 of 2-semester program leading to FREN2010.

FREN1010 is the first course inﾠthe series FREN1010, FREN1020, FREN2010 and FREN2020. It is designed to enable beginners to reach A1 level on the Common European Frame of Reference language proficiency scale. [ Click to self-assess your CEFR level ].

Students are expected to attend FREN1010 starting with the first course meeting in Week 1.ﾠ

Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice.
Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable.
Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation. Therefore, should you need
help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email
hass.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ
student email account with the following details:
Full name,
Student ID, and
the Course Code
Additional information and support can be found
here
.
course_aims: The general aim of the course is to develop a basic ability to interact with people in French and to build cultural awareness. Special emphasis is placed on speaking and understanding spoken French. The course aims to achieve the CEFR A1 (http://rm.coe.int/CoERMPublicCommonSearchServices/DisplayDCTMContent?documentId=090000168045bb52) abilities.

learning_outcomes.description: acquire French vocabulary and grammar appropriate to talking about yourself, your family and interests
interact with native and non-native speakers in basic French about your identity, daily life, interests and preferences
write and read sentences and simple texts about yourself, your family, your preferences, daily life
grow cultural awareness and openness to others in a global world
acquire strategies appropriate to learning a language

assessment_details.title: Course Placement Questionnaire
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Duration: 5-10 minutes, depending on the student's background in French (no prior knowledge of French required). It is necessary to be placed in the course prior to the end of Week 2 so that students can seek alternative courses if necessary. All students are required to demonstrate they have been correctly placed in FREN1010. Failure to do so will result in a maximum grade of 3 in the course. This is a hurdle requirement. No assessment items will be marked until this has been completed. Visit https://courseplacements.languages-cultures.uq.edu.au/ During the Course Placement questionnaire, you may be asked to write a few sentences in French. This must be your own unassisted work so we can accurately place you. This assessment task evaluates student’s abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Revision Activity
assessment_details.category: Role play/ Simulation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessment_details.due_date: 29/08/2024 5:00 pm The activity will be open from 8 am to 5 pm on Inspera.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: For this activity, using the Inspera platform, you will listen to and answer questions in French, and then read a short text in French and fill in the blanks with appropriate verbs, articles and prepositions. The interaction will be based on grammar and vocabulary from Units 1 and 2 as well as in-class materials. REQUIREMENTS: You are required to have stable access to internet, a working microphone and headset to complete this activity. You are highly encouraged to complete the Practice Test in Week 5 to test your technology. Technological problems are not a valid excuse for an extension. Duration: 45 minutes This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at a low A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Interaction and Listening. ( Link to Common European Frame of Reference ) This will be an online, timed task, completed in Inspera. You are allowed to use your book, dictionaries and typeit.org but no automatic translation software or other online resources. ** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. ** This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
online through Inspera.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.

assessment_details.title: Writing Activity
assessment_details.due_date: 19/09/2024 5:00 pm This task will be open from 8 am to 5 pm on Inspera.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In this task you will be required to demonstrate competence in writing using the grammatical structures and verb forms studied in weeks 1-8. You will write 120-180 words in response to a prompt. You will be penalised proportionately for not respecting the word limit. Duration: 25 minutes This will be an online, timed, writing task completed in Inspera. You are allowed to use your book, dictionaries and typeit.org but no automatic translation software or other online resources. You must have access to stable internet to complete this activity. ** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. ** This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Writing. ( Link to Common European Frame of Reference ) This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. assessment_details.title: Oral Interview
assessment_details.due_date: Exam Period week 1, date and time determined by sign up.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The subject of the Oral Interview will be published in week 11. In preparation, students will work in groups of two to practise topics, vocabulary and structures covered in Défi1 Units 1-4. Duration: approx 4 mns per student (groups of two students are scheduled for 10 minutes) On the day of the assessment, students will participate in a simulation run by a staff member in French. They will ask and answer questions using expressions and structures studied in the course. Students are responsible for forming a group (selecting a partner and obtaining contact details) at least two weeks prior to the assessment. The preparation work is to be completed outside of class. Students are individually marked but demonstrate ability to interact in French by working with a group. If a team member requests an extension for whatever reason, the extension will only be valid for the team member and not the whole group. An official UQ ID card is required for attendance at the final oral interview. This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Production, Spoken Interaction and Listening. ( Link to Common European Frame of Reference ) This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This Oral assessment will be recorded.

You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
This is an end of semester assessment during exam period. Approved extensions will be rescheduled based on assessors' availability.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life.
